text: It is easy to imagine that the apparent chaos of casual conversation is a degenerate form of elegantly formulated prose—yet the natural state of language is interactive conversation, not monologue.
purpose: Introduces the contrast between spoken and written language.

text: But how can such chaotic interactions ever be meaningful?
purpose: Raises a central problem or paradox following the premise.

text: Mere complex interaction surely can’t be enough in itself.
purpose: Proposes a skeptical stance toward the sufficiency of complexity for meaning.

text: Otherwise, we would have to attribute meaning to the planets in our solar system, carrying out their intricately coordinated dance in elliptical circles around the sun.
purpose: Uses an analogy to illustrate the absurdity of the previous claim.

text: Again, we shall seek the answer in the game of charades.
purpose: Signals a shift toward the forthcoming explanation or framework for meaning.
